name: Varunprasaath R. Selvaraju, Ph.D.
summary: PhD Chemist with 6+ years of materials characterization experience using advanced spectroscopic and analytical techniques. Expertise in FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR spectroscopy, thermal analysis (TGA, DSC), and thin-film materials characterization. Strong background in method development, applications support, and communicating technical concepts to diverse audiences. Experienced in surface analysis, polymer characterization, and establishing structure-property relationships. Seeking to leverage analytical expertise in a customer-facing applications scientist role supporting scientific instrumentation.

education.details:
Dissertation: Modular Design and Electrochemical Properties of Xanthene-Based Electrochromic Materials
Focus: Spectroscopy, materials characterization, thin-film analysis

education.degree: B.S. (Research), Major: Chemistry; Minor: Materials Science
education.details: INSPIRE Scholarship for Higher Education (Top 1% nationally)

bullets: Leading polymer process development with extensive materials characterization and failure mode analysis
Developing analytical methods for quality control including thermal stability assessment
Collaborating cross-functionally to establish scalable process specifications
Conducted comprehensive materials characterization using FT-IR, UV-Vis-NIR, TGA, and DSC
Identified material failure mechanisms through systematic root cause analysis
Developed physics-of-failure models and stress test protocols for material stability
Co-led AFOSR-funded process development for molecular thin-film coatings
Developed CVD-based deposition processes and surface preparation protocols
Established process control parameters and quality specifications
Optimized polymer thin-film deposition processes using DOE strategies
Established process control parameters for coating reproducibility
Managed analytical instrumentation including FT-IR, UV-Vis, TGA, and DSC systems
Implemented equipment tracking and calibration systems improving uptime by 30%
Developed standardized operating procedures (SOPs) for materials testing
Trained 15+ researchers on proper use of FT-IR, UV-Vis, and thermal analysis instrumentation, developing documentation and best practices.
Provided technical support and troubleshooting for analytical instruments, ensuring optimal performance and reliable data quality.
Mentored 120+ undergraduate students; developed instructional materials and delivered lectures on analytical techniques and spectroscopy interpretation.

skills.Spectroscopy: FT-IR (transmission, ATR), UV-Vis-NIR, Raman (familiar), ellipsometry, spectroelectrochemistry
skills.Thermal & Materials Analysis: TGA, DSC, QCM-D, NMR, GPC, cyclic voltammetry
skills.Surface & Thin-Film: Surface characterization, thin-film analysis, polymer coatings, CVD processes
skills.Applications Support Skills: Method development, customer training, technical presentations, SOP development, troubleshooting
skills.Data Analysis: Statistical analysis, DOE, JMP, Excel (advanced), spectral data processing

publications: Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Venta, R.; Rajapaksha, I.; Zhang, J.; Barlow, S.; Salman, S.; Scott, C.; Marder, S. "Narrow-Band Electrochromism of a Ferrocene-Substituted Rhodamine B." Chemistry of Materials, 2025, 37, 5558–5568.

Mandal, J.; Ramasamy Selvaraju, V.; Yan, W.; Divandari, M.; Spencer, N. D.; Dubner, M. "In Situ Monitoring of SI-ATRP Throughout Multiple Reinitiations Under Flow by Means of a Quartz Crystal Microbalance." RSC Advances, 2018, 8, 20048–20055.

projects.title: Bio-based Polymer Process Development
projects.description: Led chemical process development for novel bio-based polymer systems using statistical experimental design.

projects.title: AFOSR Defense Coatings Program
projects.description: Co-led process development for high-performance molecular thin-film coatings for defense optical applications.
